definition: Context-sensitive coverage splits high-call-count loop edges across (call-context, edge) slots, flooding the map with context-duplicate entries, diluting the reward signal for byte-level progress, and starving the mutations needed to satisfy the blocking condition.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: Context-sensitive coverage floods the edge map with (call-context, edge) pairs inside the high-call-count inflate loop, diverting mutation energy from byte changes that would produce the leftover-data condition.
signature.one_line: ctx coverage dilutes inflate-loop edges, starving the extra-data condition

analysis.summary_one_line: A valid PNG whose IDAT carries extra compressed bytes past Z_STREAM_END, reached by deep inflate-loop iteration under ordinary havoc throughput.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid PNG (IHDR/gAMA/sRGB header intact) whose IDAT stream contains residual compressed/leftover bytes so that after zlib returns Z_STREAM_END at L4237 the test png_ptr->zstream.avail_in > 0 || png_ptr->idat_size > 0 at L4245 is True. Reaching it requires driving the inflate do/while loop deep enough to hit Z_STREAM_END with leftover input.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive resolves 10/10 with ordinary havoc; its winning seed (id=7bc940e72a5dbe0c) keeps the PNG signature and IHDR intact and varies the IDAT-relevant header bytes (0x11=02/01/00, 0x17=45/46), and its lineage is pure havoc (BytesDeleteMutator/ByteRandMutator chain). naive spends its whole budget mutating bytes rather than expanding a context-indexed coverage map.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive_ctx folds a call-context hash into every edge-map slot, so the same edge counts as new coverage under each distinct caller chain. The deep IDAT loop (png_read_IDAT_data invoked 2750 times in L vs 136 in W) generates a flood of (call-context, edge) buckets that look novel, so naive_ctx hoards context-redundant corpus entries and starves the byte-level mutation that would manufacture the extra-data condition; it resolves only 1/10.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner naive is enabled by plain havoc byte mutation (havoc-only). The loser naive_ctx is penalized by its context-sensitive coverage feedback (CtxHook), which dilutes the map with (call-context, edge) pairs in the high-call-count inflate path and diverts energy away from solving the extra-compressed-data condition.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked side is the True branch of an OR test for leftover IDAT input after the deflate stream ends.
The winning side is taken only when extra compressed data remains, triggering the benign-error path at L4246.
naive resolves 10/10 and is the winner over naive_ctx via the ctx_coverage delta.
naive_ctx loses, resolving only 1/10 and staying blocked 9/10.
naive_ctx folds a call-context hash into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different slots per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: The loser invokes the enclosing IDAT function ~20x more often than the winner, showing budget burned re-traversing the inflate loop rather than resolving the branch.
value_profile resolves 8/10 and is the winner over value_profile_cmplog via the I2S delta.
value_profile_cmplog loses, resolving 0/10 and blocked 10/10.
value_profile uses naive's havoc + token stack with no I2SRandReplace, so the winner relies on plain mutation.
value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace plus a per-execution CMP buffer on top of value_profile.
The discriminating input bytes are header/IDAT-size fields at offsets 0x11 and 0x17, where the winner shows variety and the loser is uniform.
The naive winning seed keeps the PNG signature and IHDR intact, mutated only by havoc operators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Seed 1 (id=7bc940e72a5dbe0c, size=8832 bytes, fuzzer=naive, trial=1, discovered_at=12240s, mutation_op=BytesDeleteMutator,BytesRandInsertMutator):

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness reproducing the deep IDAT inflate loop with leftover compressed bytes in which naive_ctx and value_profile_cmplog resolve the extra-data branch as fast as or faster than naive/value_profile (i.e. context-coverage and I2S overhead impose no throughput/dilution tax).

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The throughput/coverage-dilution tax is inferred from invocation-count divergence and mechanism descriptions; the 0x11/0x17 byte-diff is a correlate of the extra-data condition rather than a directly verified dataflow cause of the L4245 OR test.

signature.mechanism_summary: Context-sensitive coverage splits scan-loop edges across many call-context slots, fragmenting the corpus and preventing assembly of the long CR/LF char-data run that plain havoc builds naturally.
signature.one_line: ctx coverage fragments loop edges, blocking the CR/LF char-data run

analysis.summary_one_line: Long character-data run with embedded CR/LF and printable bytes that re-iterates the do-while scan loop back to its true condition.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML element body containing a multi-character data run that keeps the do-while scan loop alive: printable ASCII / tab / newline content (0x20-0x7F, 0x09, 0x0A) plus at least one 0x0D carriage return followed by 0x0A so the in-loop CR handler executes `continue` and re-evaluates the loop condition at line 4609-4610 with `*in` still inside the accepted character class.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry long (1862/1898-byte) bodies with embedded CR/LF runs (e.g. Seed 2 bytes `0d 0d 0a` at offsets 0x07-0x09 and `0a 0a` pairs) and dense printable char data, so the loop reaches the 0xD handler and loops back to take the true side; the per-role overlay marks the 0xD/0xA continue block [W] only.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive_ctx loser-blocking seeds are short (247-527 bytes), front-loaded with binary/control noise and DTD/declaration tokens rather than long char-data bodies; they never reach the 0xD branch (loser takes it 0 times) so the loop falls through to xmlParseCharDataComplex and the true side stays blocked.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winning technique is baseline naive's undiluted havoc/token budget. naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so the same scan-loop edges count as new coverage under many distinct call paths; this fragments the corpus and spreads mutation energy across context-duplicated edges, leaving naive_ctx unable to assemble the long CR/LF-bearing char-data run that naive builds with the same mutators. This is the context-sensitive-coverage feedback acting as a penalty on the loser.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive>naive_ctx with delta ctx_coverage; naive resolves 8/10, naive_ctx 2/10.
The blocked true side sits at the do-while loop condition testing the accepted char-data byte class.
The 0xD carriage-return handler that loops back via continue is exercised only by the winner.
The loser never takes the 0xD branch while the winner takes it 138 times, and the blocker true side is taken only by the winner.
A winner-resolving seed contains embedded CR/LF bytes (0d 0d 0a) characteristic of the loop-continuing char-data run.
Loser-blocking seeds are short and front-loaded with control/binary noise rather than long char-data bodies.
naive_ctx folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different slots per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx uses the same havoc/token mutator stack as naive with no I2S or CMP_MAP, so the divergence is feedback-driven not mutation-driven.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive's havoc + token stack. No `I2SRandReplace`, no
CMP_MAP.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where adding the CtxHook context-sensitive feedback to a naive fuzzer does NOT slow discovery of a long CR/LF-bearing char-data run (naive_ctx resolves the loop-continue true side as fast as or faster than plain naive), which would show context fragmentation is not the penalizing mechanism.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The causal link from context-map fragmentation to the loser's failure to build the long char-data run is inferential; the seeds confirm the byte feature but not directly that the CtxHook is what starved naive_ctx of that mutation path.

signature.mechanism_summary: Context-sensitive coverage dilutes the edge-map signal that would reinforce the xMl name byte across call contexts, so corpus fills with context-duplicate seeds that never carry the m/M byte at name[1].
signature.one_line: ctx coverage dilutes xMl name-byte signal across call-context splits

analysis.summary_one_line: A parsed PI-target name whose first three chars are x/X,m/M,l/L (e.g. literal xMl inside <?xMl) to take name[1]=='m'/'M' true

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML processing instruction whose target name parses to a 3+ char token beginning x/X then m/M then l/L, so that name[1] is 'm' or 'M'; the winner needs the literal byte run '<?xMl' (or '<?xml') where the second name char is m/M, not an arbitrary perturbation like '<?x_l'.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The naive winner-resolving seed carries the literal bytes '<?xMl version=' at offsets 0x16-0x1d ('3c(<) 3f(?) 78(x) 4d(M) 6c(l) 20( )'), so xmlParsePITarget sees name[0]=='x',name[1]=='M',name[2]=='l' and the line 5129 true side is taken; W shows the blocker true side hit (T=3 F=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive_ctx loser seeds carry corrupted PI targets ('<?x_l' with byte 5f('_') at the name[1] position, plus xmltp/xsim variants), so name[1] is not m/M and line 5129 only ever takes the false side; the per-role overlay records the blocker exclusively as L false (T=0 F=12).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive (the winner) uses a bare per-edge MaxMapFeedback, so once havoc/token mutation lands an 'x?l'->'xMl' byte that flips line 5128 to true the new edge at 5129 is immediately rewarded. naive_ctx (the loser) folds a call-context hash into the edge-map index (CtxHook), so the same parser edges are split across many (call-context, edge) slots; the feedback signal that should guide mutation toward the xMl name is diluted across contexts, the corpus fills with context-duplicate inputs that never carry a valid xml-prefixed PI target, and the branch stays blocked in all 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the name[1]=='m'/'M' check at parser.c:5129, true side globally blocked.
The decisive pair is naive beating naive_ctx with delta ctx_coverage; naive_ctx is the loser blocked in all 10 trials.
The winner resolved 8/10 while naive_ctx resolved 0/10 and stayed blocked 10/10.
The winner-resolving seed contains the literal '<?xMl version=' byte run carrying x,M,l as the name characters.
A loser-blocking seed has a corrupted PI target '<?x_l' where the name[1] position is '_' (0x5f) rather than m/M.
On the winner the blocker true side is taken (T=3 F=0) while on the loser it is only ever false (T=0 F=12).
naive's feedback is a bare per-edge hit-count bucket with no context indexing.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx folds a call-context hash into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different map slots per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx treats a new (call-context, edge) pair as new coverage rather than a bare edge, which fragments the signal.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same x/m/l three-char PI-target gate where naive_ctx resolves the branch as fast as or faster than naive across trials, showing the context-indexed edge map does not dilute the feedback that guides mutation toward the literal name bytes.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The attribution that context-hash fragmentation (not mere run-to-run variance over a single 3-byte gate) is what blocks naive_ctx in 10/10 trials rests on mechanism reasoning rather than direct evidence of corpus context-duplication.
